reason:
Both answers correctly say no, identify the deploy pipeline (not architecture) as the bottleneck, give concrete CI/merge-queue/feature-flag fixes, invoke the Shopify/GitHub monolith-at-scale precedent, and point to modular monolith (Packwerk) as the intermediate step. Neither contains factual errors.

The differentiator is the "extract only along real team/scaling seams" key point. Answer A makes this explicit and concrete ("Extract service only when one component has genuinely different scaling/deploy needs — background job hotspot, etc.") and also spells out the specific cost of premature microservices at 15 engineers (per-person service ownership, on-call burden, tracing/mesh overhead), plus ends with a crisper, more actionable verdict. Answer B mentions modular monolith as a fallback but is thinner on when/why extraction would ever be justified, and slightly more vague about the microservices downside.

Both are concise and non-padded; the difference is modest but A covers the full set of key points a bit more completely and lands a slightly sharper actionable close.
